title: arum orientale subsp. longispathum
intro: Arum orientale subsp. longispathum, also known as the Eastern arum or Wild arum, is a perennial plant in the Arum genus, characterized by its broad, lance-shaped leaves and solitary, bell-shaped flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Arum orientale subsp. longispathum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate joint pain, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity and interactions with other medications.
